Why Shuddhi

Pressed slowly, the way it always should be

Every choice we make trades speed for purity. Here's what that means for what ends up in your kitchen.

Bull-Driven Extraction

Seeds are pressed by a wooden Ganuga turned by bulls — the original cold-press, unchanged for generations.

Chemical Free

No solvents, no hexane, no shortcuts. What leaves the press is exactly what reaches your bottle.

No Preservatives

No artificial additives keeping it "fresh." Just oil the way nature intended, from seed to bottle.

Good For Heart

Cold extraction retains natural Vitamin E and unsaturated fats that heat-based refining strips away.

Natural Nutrients

Slow, low-heat pressing preserves the aroma, colour and nutrition locked inside every seed.

Premium Quality Seeds

We hand-select every batch of sunflower, safflower, sesame and groundnut before it reaches the press.

Locally Produced

Pressed and packed at our own facility in Bishaypuram Village, Patancheru — never outsourced.

Trusted by Families

Word-of-mouth from families who switched back to pure oil — and never looked back.

Our Products

Four oils. One traditional method.

Each bottle is bull-pressed in small batches at our Bishaypuram facility, then filtered naturally and packed by hand.

Traditional Process

The bull walks in circles. So does purity.

Our Ganuga hasn't changed in generations — a bull turns a wooden press in a slow, steady circle, gently squeezing oil from the seed without heat or friction damage.

Benefits

What slow-pressing preserves

Heat and solvents used in modern refining strip away exactly what makes an oil worth having. Here's what stays intact when it's bull-pressed.

Good For Heart Health

Naturally rich in mono & poly-unsaturated fats, with zero trans fat and zero cholesterol.

Rich In Natural Vitamin E

Cold, low-heat extraction retains natural Vitamin E that high-heat refining destroys.

Chemical Free & Unrefined

No hexane, no bleaching, no deodorising — the oil you get is exactly what the seed gave up.

Natural Goodness, Batch After Batch

Every bottle carries the same aroma, colour and nutrition our family has trusted for generations.

About Shuddhi

A promise made in Bishaypuram Village

Shuddhi Eddhu Ganuga Oils began with a simple discomfort — watching kitchens fill with oils nobody's grandmother would recognise. Refined, deodorised, stripped of aroma, and pressed at speeds that generate more heat than any seed should see.

So we went back to the Ganuga — the wooden, bull-driven press our village has used for generations. It's slower. It costs more. It asks patience of both the bull and us. But it keeps every oil exactly as nature made it: unrefined, chemical-free, and full of the aroma our elders remember.

Every bottle is pressed, filtered and packed by hand at our own facility in Bishaypuram Village, Patancheru — not outsourced, not mass-produced. Just a family business, working the same way it always has, for families who want the same.

FAQ

Good questions, honest answers

Why bull-driven, when machines are faster?

Electric expellers spin fast and generate heat, which changes the oil's natural aroma, colour and nutrient content. A bull-driven Ganuga turns slowly and gently, keeping the temperature low so the oil stays as close as possible to how the seed naturally holds it.

What exactly is Ganuga Oil?

"Ganuga" is the traditional wooden oil press used across our region, turned by a bull walking in a slow circle. Ganuga Oil simply means oil extracted this way — unrefined, cold-pressed, and free of chemical processing.

What is the shelf life?

Our oils have a shelf life of 12 months from the date of packing, as noted on every bottle's label along with the batch number and packed-on date.

How should I store the oil?

Store in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight. Since our oils are unrefined and free of preservatives, keeping them away from heat and light helps preserve their natural quality for longer.

Do you deliver, and where?

Yes — message us on WhatsApp with your location and we'll confirm delivery timelines and charges for your area, along with the sizes currently available.

What payment options are available?

We accept UPI, bank transfer and cash on delivery in select areas. Payment details are shared once your order is confirmed over WhatsApp.
